title: Pre/post evaluation instrument (appraisal rubric + confidence survey)
context: The project's primary outcome metrics are a measurable gain in advocates' appraisal ability (>= 25-point median gain on a 100-pt rubric) and self-rated confidence (+= 2 on a 5-pt scale). These require a validated pre/post evaluation instrument. The instrument must be designed carefully so it can detect genuine skill gain, not familiarity with the specific papers used in the walkthrough examples. A methodology reviewer must sign off.
objective: Design and author a pre/post evaluation instrument comprising a blinded appraisal rubric and a validated confidence survey, capable of measuring the skill-gain and confidence-gain outcome metrics specified in PLAN.md.
acceptanceCriteria: The appraisal rubric is scored on a 100-point scale; it applies to a novel annotated-paper exercise distinct from the walkthrough examples used in training, so pre/post scoring is not contaminated by familiarity.
The rubric covers the core appraisal domains taught in the curriculum: study design identification, endpoint interpretation, effect-size reading, bias identification, and COI/funding assessment.
The confidence survey uses a validated PPI confidence instrument or an original survey validated against it, with a 5-point scale per domain.
Instrument is usable both pre-pilot (before any training) and post-pilot (immediately after); administration instructions are included.
The paper used in the rubric exercise is licence-verified (CC-licensed OA, recorded in sources.yaml) and distinct from the M0/M2 walkthrough papers.
Methodology reviewer sign-off recorded: instrument design is capable of detecting the target skill gains and is free from obvious bias or floor/ceiling effects.
Advocate reviewer sign-off recorded: instrument is accessible and usable by lay advocates.
resources: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/tools/openftlist/ (PMC OA Subset — for the rubric exercise paper)
output: A pre/post evaluation instrument document (governance/evaluation-instrument.md or equivalent) committed to the repo, comprising an appraisal rubric and confidence survey with administration instructions, carrying methodology + advocate review sign-offs.
